message:
fix(simulations): Add double buffering and infection time tracking to plaque CA

Major plaque automata simulation improvements:

Double buffering for correct CA semantics:
- Previous: Single-pass update caused sequential artifacts
  - Cells updated early in loop affected later cells in same tick
  - Created directional bias in infection spread
- Now: Read from currentGrid, write to nextGrid
  - All decisions based on state at tick start
  - No within-tick interference between cells
  - Proper cellular automata behavior

Infection time tracking:
- Add infectionTimes Float32Array parallel to grid
- Track actual age of each infected cell
- Properly increment age each tick for infected cells
- Lysis decision based on accumulated age >= latent period
- More biologically realistic: infection duration varies by cell

Counting improvements:
- Recount phage/bacteria/infected from final grid state
- Previous code tried to track deltas but was error-prone
- Fresh count ensures display accuracy

State transition fixes:
- Free phage diffusion now uses nextGrid properly
- Infection events update both nextGrid and nextAges
- Lysis burst distributes to empty nextGrid cells
- Bacteria regrowth checks nextGrid state

These changes make the plaque simulation behave like a proper
reaction-diffusion system with consistent update semantics.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
